description: HP 5342A Microwave Frequency Counter (10 Hz to 18 GHz) with HP-IB Interface Option 011. Uses a function-code (program-code) remote language. Each complete program code is executed by the microprocessor as if entered from the front panel, so codes in a string must appear in the same order as front-panel entry. Provides automatic/manual frequency mode, manual center frequency, amplitude measurement, frequency and amplitude offsets (including automatic offsets), resolution and range selection, FM/CW mode, four sample-rate (trigger/hold) modes, and output-mode control. Measurement data is returned in fixed-length ASCII formats terminated by CR LF.

commands.name: frequency_mode_auto
commands.description: Frequency Mode Select - Automatic. Selects the automatic frequency acquisition mode (analogous to the front panel AUTO operation).

commands.name: frequency_mode_manual
commands.description: Frequency Mode Select - Manual. Selects manual frequency mode; the center frequency is set with the SM (Set Manual Center Frequency) command.

commands.name: set_manual_center_frequency
commands.description: Set Manual Center Frequency. Sets the manual center frequency. The data string is a nonfixed-length string of up to 5 characters in MHz, terminated by E. Decimal points cause the entire string to be ignored; + signs and spaces are allowed. The number must be less than 18 GHz or it is ignored.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: up to 5 characters, must be < 18000 (18 GHz); decimal points cause the string to be ignored

commands.name: amplitude_mode_off
commands.description: Amplitude Mode Select - Off. Turns the amplitude measurement off (frequency only).

commands.name: amplitude_mode_on
commands.description: Amplitude Mode Select - On. Turns the amplitude measurement on; both frequency and amplitude are measured and output.

commands.name: frequency_offset_off
commands.description: Frequency Offset Mode Select - Off. Disables the frequency offset.

commands.name: frequency_offset_on
commands.description: Frequency Offset Mode Select - On. Enables the frequency offset set with the SOM command.

commands.name: set_frequency_offset
commands.description: Set Frequency Offset. Sets the frequency offset value. The data string is a nonfixed-length signed value in MHz terminated by E. Spaces are ignored.

commands.name: amplitude_offset_off
commands.description: Amplitude Offset Mode - Off. Disables the amplitude offset.

commands.name: amplitude_offset_on
commands.description: Amplitude Offset Mode - On. Enables the amplitude offset set with the SOB command.

commands.name: set_amplitude_offset
commands.description: Set Amplitude Offset. Sets the amplitude offset value. The data string is a nonfixed-length signed value in dB terminated by E. Spaces are ignored.

commands.name: resolution
commands.description: Resolution. Selects the measurement resolution. SR3=1 Hz, SR4=10 Hz, SR5=100 Hz, SR6=1 kHz, SR7=10 kHz, SR8=100 kHz, SR9=1 MHz. (Note: SR1 is the separate Check Mode code.)

commands.name: range_low
commands.description: Range Select - Low. Selects the 10 Hz to 500 MHz range.

commands.name: range_high
commands.description: Range Select - High. Selects the 500 MHz to 18 GHz range.

commands.name: cw_mode
commands.description: FM/CW Mode - CW. Selects CW (continuous wave) measurement mode.

commands.name: fm_mode
commands.description: FM/CW Mode - FM. Selects FM measurement mode.

commands.name: sample_rate_front_panel
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Front panel. Sample rate is determined by the front panel SAMPLE RATE control.

commands.name: sample_rate_hold
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Hold. Places the counter in hold; a trigger message (e.g. trg 7) must be sent to start a measurement. If the 5342A is in remote and addressed to listen and not already in Hold (T1), a trigger command automatically puts it into Sample-then-Hold (T3).

commands.name: sample_rate_fast
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Fast sample (no delay). The counter ignores any sample-rate run-down and initiates a new measurement as soon as the previous measurement is over.

commands.name: sample_rate_sample_then_hold
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Sample then Hold. The counter takes a measurement and holds until the next trigger message.

commands.name: output_mode_only_when_addressed
commands.description: Output Mode - Output only when addressed. At the end of a measurement the counter pulls SRQ and checks if it has been addressed to talk; if not, it clears SRQ and starts the next measurement. If addressed to talk it outputs the measurement, clears SRQ, and starts the next measurement.

commands.name: output_mode_wait_until_addressed
commands.description: Output Mode - Wait until addressed. At the end of a measurement the counter pulls SRQ and waits in a loop until addressed to talk, then outputs the measurement, clears SRQ, and starts the next measurement. Use this mode when triggering in HOLD (T1) and then addressing to talk.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset. Blanks the display and initiates a new measurement. If in Hold (T1) the measurement is not completed but stays in Hold. Does not return control to local.

commands.name: automatic_frequency_offset
commands.description: Automatic Frequency Offset. Sets the frequency offset automatically to the current measured value.

commands.name: automatic_amplitude_offset
commands.description: Automatic Amplitude Offset. Sets the amplitude offset automatically to the current measured value.

commands.name: check_mode
commands.description: Check Mode. Internal check function. No input may be present at the RF connector and the counter must be set to SAMPLE RATE full counter-clockwise. Send the RESET command (RE) before making other measurements.
